question: What were the main challenges in my distributed file storage system?

relevant_units.desc: System overview and 3-layer design (client, metadata via chain replication, storage via consistent hash ring).
Replication for node failure recovery; automated tests that killed nodes mid-operation and verified recovery.
Hash comparison at retrieval time to verify reconstructed file matches original; consistent hashing for even distribution.
TCP message-boundary bug and length-prefix protocol fix — told 3 times across the knowledge base.
Known limitations (rejoin handling, no persistent storage, hardcoded config) and protocol-level debugging reflection.
Why this project matters — demonstrates distributed-systems fundamentals and reliability mindset.

expected_facts: fault tolerance / recovery from replicas during node failures
data integrity via hash comparison
the TCP message-boundary bug solved with a length-prefix protocol
notes: 6 units (10 chunks). Dedicated consolidated doc plus original scattered fragments. distributed-storage__c3 assigned to limitations (length-prefix story well-covered by 3 KB chunks).
